message:
style+test: rustfmt ping-pong reverts + e2e coverage for `cass models backfill --scheduled`

Two trivial format reverts plus a substantive e2e test landing
together because a concurrent agent bundled them.

src/html_export/encryption.rs:
- Import groups flip back AGAIN between the two equivalent
  rustfmt-canonical forms (`aead::{Aead, KeyInit}, Aes256Gcm, Nonce`
  vs `Aes256Gcm, Nonce, aead::{...}`, and `base64::Engine` vs
  `base64::prelude::BASE64_STANDARD` ordering). This is pure
  rustfmt-ping-pong between concurrent agents on different
  rustfmt configs. Commit what the current pass produced so the
  working tree stops reporting dirty.

src/indexer/semantic.rs:
- One test assertion splits across lines to satisfy rustfmt's
  line-width rule:
    assert_eq!(lexical_decision.state, SemanticBackfillSchedulerState::Paused);
  becomes the multi-line form. No content change.

src/lib.rs:
- The "Next eligible after" println inside the `run_models_backfill`
  pause-status branch splits across three lines for the same
  rustfmt reason. Output is byte-identical.

tests/e2e_semantic_backfill_robot.rs (new):
- End-to-end coverage for the just-landed `cass models backfill
  --scheduled` gate. Constructs a FrankenStorage fixture with one
  Codex-agent conversation + one user message, seeds a
  SemanticManifest on the tier surface, then invokes the real
  `cass` binary via `assert_cmd::cargo::cargo_bin_cmd` with the
  `--scheduled` flag set AND
  `CASS_SEMANTIC_BACKFILL_FOREGROUND_ACTIVE=1` in the env so the
  scheduler decision deterministically resolves to
  Paused/ForegroundPressure. Parses the robot JSON output and
  asserts the `state`, `reason`, and `scheduled_batch_conversations`
  fields match what the unit tests already pin on the pure policy
  function. Complements the internal coverage with a real-binary
  round-trip.

No runtime behavior change from the style edits; new test file
adds integration coverage only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
